description: Run 001 baseline official n=4 Laplace hard-backoff n-gram

asi.hypothesis: Record the official template baseline as the first machine-logged autoresearch run before changing predictors.
asi.source: course material + official project files + existing explanation run_001
asi.learned: Baseline reproduced exactly at 2.9986251144 bits/symbol with full 200000 tokens and checks passed.
asi.next_focus: Try the first classical n-gram hyperparameter change: n=5 with Laplace=1.0 and online adaptation.

asi.hypothesis: A one-symbol-longer context may reduce conditional model mismatch if source memory extends beyond the n=4 baseline.
asi.source: autoresearch ideas backlog + competition rules optional n=5 reference + lectures 7 and 13 on ideal code lengths/history as probability evidence
asi.learned: Increasing hard-backoff order from n=4 to n=5 with Laplace=1.0 worsened public-practice loss from 2.9986251144 to 3.1231208254, despite passing checks and running slightly faster. Sparse high-order contexts and/or over-smoothing dominate any extra-memory benefit.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.1245 bits/symbol versus baseline, so this candidate should not replace the official template.
asi.next_action_hint: Try n=5 with smaller Laplace such as 0.1 or add thresholded backoff/min_count before trusting high-order contexts.

asi.hypothesis: Lowering n=5 pseudocount from 1.0 to 0.1 may reduce over-smoothing while keeping positive support.
asi.source: run 002 observation + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5 and 13
asi.learned: n=5 with Laplace=0.1 worsened further to 3.2288198643, so the plain hard-backoff n=5 problem is not just over-smoothing; sparse length-4 contexts and hard context selection are likely harmful.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was worse than both baseline and run 002, despite checks passing.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop plain n=5 hard-backoff sweeps for now; either tune n=4 smoothing or introduce thresholded backoff so high-order contexts require minimum support.

asi.hypothesis: At the proven n=4 context order, lowering the pseudocount to 0.1 may reduce over-smoothing while preserving positive support.
asi.source: run 001 baseline + run 002/003 n=5 failures + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5 and 7
asi.learned: n=4 with Laplace=0.1 worsened to 3.0358045164, so the baseline's Laplace=1.0 is not obviously too large; sharper smoothing underweights surprising symbols in public practice.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.0372 bits/symbol versus baseline, despite checks passing.
asi.next_action_hint: Try a gentler n=4 smoothing change such as Laplace=0.5 or test adapt_online=False; avoid very low pseudocounts for hard-backoff n-grams.

asi.hypothesis: A moderate n=4 pseudocount 0.5 may reduce over-smoothing without the surprise penalty of 0.1.
asi.source: run 001 baseline + run 004 low-smoothing failure + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5 and 7
asi.learned: n=4 Laplace=0.5 scored 3.0001517271, only slightly worse than baseline but still not an improvement. Laplace=1.0 remains the best tested smoothing for hard-backoff n=4.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was worse than baseline by about 0.00153 bits/symbol, so no keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Try adapt_online=False to test whether online adaptation hurts, or move to thresholded n=5/min_count to address hard selection sparsity rather than more n=4 smoothing.

description: Run 006 n=4 static train-only hard-backoff n-gram

asi.hypothesis: Disabling online adaptation may prevent test-prefix noise from moving the train-derived n=4 model away from the source conditional law.
asi.source: run 001/004/005 observations + autoresearch ideas backlog + harness update ordering + lectures 5 and 13
asi.learned: Static n=4 Laplace=1.0 worsened to 3.0109261870 while running faster, so online adaptation materially improves log-loss on public practice despite its small runtime cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.0123 bits/symbol versus baseline, even though runtime improved.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep online adaptation enabled. Move to thresholded n=5/min_count or mixture/backoff variants rather than disabling updates.

asi.hypothesis: Require at least 8 observations before using nonzero-order contexts in n=5, so sparse high-order histories do not dominate predictions.
asi.source: competition rules optional ngram_threshold reference + runs 002/003 n=5 failures + run 006 adaptation result + lectures 5 and 13
asi.learned: Thresholding all nonzero contexts at min_count=8 with n=5 Laplace=1.0 still worsened to 3.0626460725. This implementation likely backs off too aggressively at some lower orders or high-order contexts are not useful under Laplace=1.0.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus baseline by about 0.0640 bits/symbol, despite checks passing.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider thresholding only the new order-4 contexts (keeping n=4 behavior unchanged) or move to mixture-of-orders so lower-order evidence is never discarded.

description: Run 008 threshold only length-4 n=5 contexts min_count=8

asi.hypothesis: Preserve baseline n=4 fallback and use length-4 contexts only when total count >=8, recovering any useful longer-memory signal without changing lower-order behavior.
asi.source: run 007 thresholding failure + runs 002/003 n=5 failures + competition rules optional reference + lectures 5 and 13
asi.learned: Thresholding only the extra length-4 context still worsened to 3.0655748167, about the same as thresholding all nonzero orders. Length-4 hard-context estimates appear harmful on public practice even when supported by at least 8 observations.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.06695 bits/symbol versus baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop n=5 hard/thresholded context experiments for now. Move to mixtures of orders 0..3/4 so lower-order evidence remains in the probability distribution rather than being discarded.

description: Run 009 count-dependent interpolated n=4 n-gram C=16

asi.hypothesis: Recursive count-dependent interpolation over orders 0..3 can reduce hard-backoff model-order mismatch while staying close to reliable high-order counts.
asi.source: runs 002-008 dead ends + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5, 13, and 15 on cross-entropy, recurrence evidence, and mixture universal codes
asi.learned: Interpolated n=4 with C=16 improved public-practice loss from 2.9986251144 to 2.9817296585, a 0.0169 bits/symbol gain, while checks passed. Mixture/model averaging over context orders is the first successful direction; runtime increased to 3.59s but remains far below the 600s limit.
asi.next_focus: Tune the interpolation constant C around 8, 16, 32 and consider n=5 interpolated only after validating n=4 mixture robustness.

asi.hypothesis: A larger interpolation constant C=32 may improve calibration by retaining lower-order evidence longer than C=16.
asi.source: run 009 improvement + runs 002-008 dead ends + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and mixture universal codes
asi.learned: Increasing interpolation constant from C=16 to C=32 improved loss further from 2.9817296585 to 2.9794649970, suggesting the model benefits from more conservative high-order weighting. Runtime stayed safe at 3.61s.
asi.next_focus: Try C=64 or C=24/48 to locate the interpolation optimum; later validate the best interpolated model on train-derived splits.

asi.hypothesis: A larger interpolation constant C=64 may further reduce over-trust in high-order contexts after C=32 improved over C=16.
asi.source: run 009/010 interpolation improvements + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and universal mixture codes
asi.learned: C=64 scored 2.9798657316, slightly worse than current best C=32 at 2.9794649970 but still better than baseline. The interpolation optimum is likely near C=32 rather than much larger; C=64 was also a bit faster/noisy in runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve over the current best C=32 model, worsening by about 0.00040 bits/symbol.
asi.next_action_hint: Try intermediate constants around C=24 or C=48 to bracket the optimum, or rerun C=32 for confirmation because confidence remains low.

asi.hypothesis: An intermediate interpolation constant C=48 may bracket the optimum between current best C=32 and slightly worse C=64.
asi.source: run 010 C=32 success + run 011 C=64 bracket + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and universal mixture codes
asi.learned: C=48 improved slightly over C=32, scoring 2.9792935749 versus 2.9794649970, and was faster in this run at 3.20s. The interpolation optimum is around 32-64, with C=48 current best; effect size is tiny and likely requires confirmation.
asi.next_focus: Try nearby constants C=40 or C=56, and rerun current best C=48/C=32 for noise confirmation before broader model changes.

asi.hypothesis: C=40 may improve over C=32/C=48 if the interpolation optimum lies between them.
asi.source: run 010 C=32, run 012 C=48, run 011 C=64 + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and universal mixture codes
asi.learned: C=40 produced a tiny new best at 2.9792409029, slightly better than C=48 by about 0.000053 bits/symbol, with faster observed runtime 3.05s. The optimum appears around 40-48, but gains are well within noise and require confirmation.
asi.next_focus: Try C=36 or rerun C=40/C=48 for confirmation; consider consolidating interpolated predictor to avoid many duplicate files after tuning.

asi.hypothesis: C=36 may improve if the interpolation optimum lies between C=32 and current best C=40.
asi.source: run 010 C=32, run 013 C=40, run 012 C=48 + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and universal mixture codes
asi.learned: C=36 scored 2.9793094026, worse than current best C=40 at 2.9792409029 and close to C=48. The local optimum is likely around C=40, but differences remain very small and need confirmation.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.0000685 bits/symbol versus current best C=40.
asi.next_action_hint: Try C=44 or rerun C=40 to confirm; if no clear gain, consolidate the interpolated predictor and build train-derived validation.

asi.hypothesis: C=44 may improve if the local interpolation optimum lies between current best C=40 and prior C=48.
asi.source: run 013 C=40, run 014 C=36, run 012 C=48 + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and universal mixture codes
asi.learned: C=44 scored 2.9792403269, a vanishingly small improvement over C=40 by about 0.000000576 bits/symbol. The optimum is flat/noise-dominated around C=40-44; further constant tuning is unlikely to be meaningful without repeats or validation.
asi.next_focus: Rerun current best C=44 or C=40 for noise confirmation, then consolidate duplicate interpolated predictors or build train-derived validation.

asi.hypothesis: Soft interpolation may use length-4 context evidence safely, unlike hard/thresholded n=5, while preserving lower-order mass.
asi.source: run 015 current n=4 interpolated best + runs 002/003/007/008 n=5 hard failures + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5, 13, and 15
asi.learned: Interpolated n=5 with C=44 scored 2.9848362778, much worse than n=4 C=44 but still better than baseline. Adding length-4 contexts hurts even under soft interpolation; order-4 information appears noisy/mismatched for public practice.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.005596 bits/symbol versus current best n=4 C=44, and runtime increased to 4.42s.
asi.next_action_hint: Stay with n=4 interpolated models. Next useful step is confirmation/validation or consolidation rather than more n=5 context experiments.

description: Run 017 interpolated n=4 C=44 online weight 2

asi.hypothesis: Doubling online updates may adapt faster to the evaluated stream if online evidence is underweighted relative to train counts.
asi.source: run 006 static failure + run 015 current n=4 interpolated best + run 016 n=5 dead end + autoresearch ideas backlog + lectures 5, 13, and 15
asi.learned: Online update weight 2 scored 2.9801459465, worse than the current best 2.9792403269 though still better than baseline. Online adaptation helps versus static, but doubling each observation overweights sequential noise or recent realization details.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000906 bits/symbol versus n=4 C=44 with unit online updates.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not increase online weight above 1 as a simple multiplier. If revisiting adaptation, try gentler fractional/static-online mixtures or validate on train-derived splits first.

asi.hypothesis: KT-style half-count smoothing may sharpen local interpolated context estimates while lower-order mixture mass protects against rare surprises.
asi.source: runs 009-015 interpolation success + run 016/017 dead ends + run 005 hard-backoff Laplace 0.5 near miss + agent prior knowledge about KT/Jeffreys smoothing + lectures 5 and 15
asi.learned: Laplace 0.5 with n=4 C=44 improved to 2.9773145652, about 0.001926 bits/symbol better than the prior best. Interpolation makes sharper half-count local estimates useful, unlike hard-backoff where Laplace 0.5 was slightly worse.
asi.next_focus: Bracket smoothing around 0.5, e.g. 0.25 or 0.75, or confirm this model on train-derived validation/repeat runs before further public-practice tuning.

asi.hypothesis: Lowering additive smoothing from 0.5 to 0.25 may sharpen reliable local context estimates while interpolation protects rare cases.
asi.source: run 018 half-count smoothing improvement + run 004 very-low hard-backoff smoothing failure + run 016/017 dead ends + lectures 5 and 15 + symmetric Dirichlet prior reasoning
asi.learned: Additive smoothing 0.25 scored 2.9780370195, worse than 0.5 but still better than add-one. The smoothing optimum is likely near the KT/Jeffreys half-count rather than much lower; too-sharp local estimates begin to underweight surprises.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000722 bits/symbol versus current best Laplace 0.5.
asi.next_action_hint: Try the upper bracket Laplace 0.75 or retune interpolation C around the Laplace 0.5 model, but avoid much smaller pseudocounts.

asi.hypothesis: Additive smoothing 0.75 may be a better upper bracket if half-count smoothing is slightly too sharp but add-one is too conservative.
asi.source: run 018 half-count improvement + run 019 lower-smoothing failure + run 015 add-one baseline for same structure + lectures 5 and 15 + symmetric Dirichlet prior reasoning
asi.learned: Additive smoothing 0.75 scored 2.9779357716, worse than 0.5 but better than add-one. Together with 0.25, this brackets the smoothing optimum around KT/Jeffreys half-count for the n=4 C=44 interpolated model.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000621 bits/symbol versus current best Laplace 0.5.
asi.next_action_hint: Retune interpolation C for Laplace 0.5, or prioritize train-derived validation/repeat confirmation before further narrow public-practice tuning.

asi.hypothesis: Increasing interpolation C to 56 may compensate for sharper half-count local estimates by retaining lower-order mixture mass longer.
asi.source: run 018 current best half-count model + runs 019/020 smoothing bracket + runs 011-015 add-one C bracket + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and robust mixtures
asi.learned: C=56 with Laplace 0.5 scored 2.9775503820, worse than C=44 but still close. The half-count model does not benefit from substantially more conservative high-order weighting; the C optimum may remain near 40-44 or slightly lower.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000236 bits/symbol versus current best C=44 Laplace 0.5.
asi.next_action_hint: If retuning C further, try C=36 or C=40 with Laplace 0.5; otherwise prioritize validation/confirmation over more narrow public-practice tuning.

asi.hypothesis: Lowering interpolation C to 40 may trust reliable high-order contexts slightly sooner under half-count smoothing.
asi.source: run 018 current best + run 021 C=56 worse + add-one C=40/C=44 flat from runs 013/015 + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and robust mixtures
asi.learned: C=40 with Laplace 0.5 scored 2.9773386624, very close but slightly worse than C=44. The half-count interpolation optimum remains around C=44; both higher C=56 and lower C=40 miss by small margins.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000024 bits/symbol versus current best C=44 Laplace 0.5.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop narrow C/smoothing tuning for now; prioritize train-derived validation/repeat confirmation or a qualitatively different sequential mixture such as fixed global weights or PPM-style escape.

description: Run 023 n=4 half-count adaptive unique-count interpolation

asi.hypothesis: Using continuation diversity T as context-specific interpolation uncertainty may trust concentrated high-order contexts sooner and ambiguous contexts later.
asi.source: run 018 current best + runs 019-022 saturated fixed smoothing/C tuning + autoresearch ideas backlog PPM-style escape + lectures 5, 13, and 15
asi.learned: Adaptive C_eff=4*T with Laplace 0.5 improved to 2.9771815925, about 0.000133 bits/symbol better than fixed C=44. Continuation diversity is a useful uncertainty signal beyond total count alone, though the gain is small and needs validation.
asi.next_focus: Bracket the adaptive scale around 4, e.g. 3 or 5/6, or validate current best on train-derived splits before more public-practice tuning.

asi.hypothesis: Lowering adaptive diversity scale to 3 may trust concentrated high-order contexts sooner than run 023 scale 4.
asi.source: run 023 adaptive C_eff=4*T improvement + runs 019-022 saturated fixed tuning + PPM-style escape idea backlog + lectures 5, 13, and 15
asi.learned: Adaptive scale 3 scored 2.9774736781, worse than scale 4. The run 023 model was not too cautious; smaller diversity penalty overtrusts context-specific counts.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000292 bits/symbol versus adaptive scale 4.
asi.next_action_hint: Try the upper bracket scale 5 or 6 once, or validate run 023 before more public-practice scale tuning.

asi.hypothesis: Increasing adaptive diversity scale to 5 may reserve more lower-order mass if run 023 scale 4 still overtrusts ambiguous contexts.
asi.source: run 023 adaptive C_eff=4*T improvement + run 024 scale 3 failure + PPM-style escape idea backlog + lectures 5, 13, and 15
asi.learned: Adaptive scale 5 scored 2.9774876940, worse than scale 4 and about the same as scale 3. Scale 4 is bracketed by worse lower and upper settings; continuation-diversity scale tuning should stop for now.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000306 bits/symbol versus adaptive scale 4.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop adaptive scale fine-tuning. Prioritize train-derived validation/repeat confirmation of run 023 or try a qualitatively different sequential model such as full Witten-Bell/PPM escape.

description: Run 026 PPM-style adaptive MLE local counts

asi.hypothesis: Replacing smoothed higher-order local distributions with MLE counts plus diversity-based escape may let lower-order models handle unseen symbols better.
asi.source: run 023 adaptive scale 4 best + runs 024/025 scale bracket failures + PPM-style escape backlog + lectures 5, 13, and 15
asi.learned: PPM-style MLE local components scored 2.9832630018, much worse than the smoothed adaptive run 023. Half-count smoothing inside higher-order local distributions remains important; escape/backoff alone does not provide enough robustness.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.006081 bits/symbol versus adaptive half-count scale 4.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid unsmoothed MLE local distributions. If exploring PPM further, keep half-count local smoothing or use exclusion carefully; otherwise prioritize validation/confirmation of run 023.

description: Run 027 equal mixture fixed-C and adaptive-C half-count n-gram

asi.hypothesis: An equal probability mixture of fixed-C44 and adaptive C_eff=4*T half-count predictors may hedge complementary calibration errors.
asi.source: run 018 fixed-C half-count model + run 023 adaptive continuation-diversity model + run 026 MLE local failure + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and robust mixture coding
asi.learned: The 50/50 model mixture improved to 2.9771224184, about 0.000059 bits/symbol better than adaptive scale 4 alone. Fixed-count and diversity-adaptive confidence schedules have complementary local calibration errors; explicit model averaging helps, at the cost of runtime rising to about 5.0s.
asi.next_focus: Validate or repeat the model-mixture best before tuning mixture weights. If continuing, try a coarse weight such as 0.25 fixed / 0.75 adaptive rather than many fine weights.

asi.hypothesis: Weighting the model mixture 25% fixed-C44 and 75% adaptive C_eff=4*T may keep the adaptive model's strength while preserving a smaller fixed-C hedge.
asi.source: run 027 equal model mixture best + run 023 adaptive model stronger than fixed run 018 + lectures 5 and 15 on cross-entropy mismatch and robust mixture coding
asi.learned: The 25/75 fixed/adaptive mixture scored 2.9771224575, effectively tied with but fractionally worse than the 50/50 mixture by about 0.000000039 bits/symbol. Equal weighting remains best within numerical/noise-level resolution; the coarse weight change did not matter materially.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve over current best run 027; the difference is negligible but non-improving.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop public-practice mixture-weight tuning. Prioritize train-derived validation/repeat confirmation of run 027 or a new qualitatively different model.

description: Run 029 n=5 fixed/adaptive half-count model mixture

asi.hypothesis: The robust fixed/adaptive half-count mixture may safely use length-4 context evidence that earlier n=5 models overtrusted.
asi.source: run 027 current best model mixture + earlier n=5 failures with simpler confidence mechanisms + lectures 5, 13, and 15 on context evidence, mismatch, and robust mixtures
asi.learned: Extending the fixed/adaptive half-count model mixture to n=5 improved to 2.9766819653, a 0.000440 bits/symbol gain over n=4 run 027. Earlier n=5 failures were due to insufficiently robust confidence/smoothing, not necessarily useless length-4 context information.
asi.next_focus: Bracket n=5 model mixture details cautiously, e.g. test n=6 with the same robust mechanism or validate n=5/n=4 on train-derived splits before more public-practice tuning.

description: Run 030 n=6 fixed/adaptive half-count model mixture

asi.hypothesis: The robust fixed/adaptive half-count mixture may safely use length-5 context evidence if the source has memory beyond four symbols.
asi.source: run 029 n=5 robust mixture improvement + earlier n=5 failures explained by insufficient robustness + lectures 5, 13, and 15 on context evidence, mismatch, and robust mixtures
asi.learned: n=6 fixed/adaptive mixture scored 2.9965394663, much worse than n=5 and only slightly better than baseline. Length-5 contexts are too sparse/noisy even with robust confidence and half-count smoothing; n=5 is the useful higher-order limit for this family.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.019858 bits/symbol versus current best n=5 model mixture.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not increase order beyond n=5 in this family. Next try validating n=5 or retuning n=5 confidence/smoothing coarsely, not n=6+.

asi.hypothesis: In the n=5 robust model mixture, local smoothing 0.4 may sharpen informative high-order contexts while fixed/adaptive averaging protects surprises.
asi.source: run 029 current best n=5 model mixture + run 030 n=6 order limit failure + n=4 smoothing bracket from runs 018-020 + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and robust mixtures
asi.learned: Lowering local smoothing from 0.5 to 0.4 in the n=5 model mixture improved to 2.9754042414, a sizable 0.001278 bits/symbol gain. The n=5 robust mixture benefits from sharper local distributions than the n=4 family.
asi.next_focus: Bracket n=5 smoothing around 0.4, e.g. try 0.3 or 0.45/0.35, while being mindful of public-practice overfitting and validating soon.

asi.hypothesis: Further lowering n=5 model-mixture local smoothing to 0.3 may sharpen informative high-order contexts if model averaging protects rare outcomes.
asi.source: run 031 smoothing 0.4 improvement + run 030 n=6 limit failure + n=4 smoothing bracket + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and robust mixtures
asi.learned: Lowering n=5 model-mixture smoothing to 0.3 improved to 2.9749371262, another 0.000467 bits/symbol gain. The robust n=5 mixture continues to benefit from sharper local distributions, but lower smoothing increases risk of underweighting surprises and should be bracketed cautiously.
asi.next_focus: Try a lower bracket such as Laplace 0.2 or an intermediate 0.25, then validate/repeat the best to avoid overfitting public practice.

asi.hypothesis: Lowering n=5 model-mixture smoothing to 0.2 may further sharpen informative contexts if mixture backoff protects surprises.
asi.source: run 032 smoothing 0.3 improvement + run 031 smoothing 0.4 improvement + run 030 n=6 limit failure + lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and robust mixtures
asi.learned: Laplace 0.2 scored 2.9759198844, worse than 0.3 and 0.4. The n=5 smoothing optimum is bracketed between 0.2 and 0.4, with 0.3 current best; too little smoothing underweights surprises in sparse high-order contexts.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000983 bits/symbol versus current best Laplace 0.3.
asi.next_action_hint: If continuing smoothing, try intermediate 0.25 or 0.35 once; otherwise validate/repeat n=5 Laplace 0.3.

description: Run 034 n=5 diversity-dependent smoothing model mixture

asi.hypothesis: Diversity-dependent smoothing may keep sharp estimates for low-diversity contexts while avoiding the global Laplace 0.2 surprise penalty in high-diversity contexts.
asi.source: run 032 Laplace 0.3 best, run 033 Laplace 0.2 worse, runs 023-025 continuation-diversity signal, lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and robust mixtures
asi.learned: The diversity-dependent smoothing formula scored 2.9752131102: better than global 0.2 but worse than global 0.3. Continuation diversity alone is not enough to choose smoothing; it likely sharpens sparse low-diversity contexts too much or over-smooths genuinely useful high-diversity contexts.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000276 bits/symbol versus current best global Laplace 0.3.
asi.next_action_hint: Prefer current n=5 Laplace 0.3. If continuing, try one conservative smoothing ensemble such as mixing alpha 0.3 with alpha 0.4, or move to train-derived validation rather than more scalar tuning.

description: Run 035 n=5 smoothing ensemble alpha 0.3 and 0.4

asi.hypothesis: A 50/50 smoothing ensemble over alpha 0.3 and alpha 0.4 may hedge calibration errors while preserving the current best sharper model.
asi.source: run 032 alpha 0.3 current best, run 031 alpha 0.4 close, run 033 alpha 0.2 too sharp, run 034 adaptive smoothing worse, lectures 5 and 15 on mismatch and universal mixtures
asi.learned: The alpha 0.3/0.4 ensemble scored 2.9750329918, slightly worse than alpha 0.3 alone. The conservative alpha 0.4 component dilutes the best model more than it protects against surprises on this benchmark, and runtime nearly doubles to 9.6s.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000096 bits/symbol versus current best alpha 0.3.
asi.next_action_hint: Prefer the simple n=5 alpha 0.3 fixed/adaptive mixture. Further public-practice smoothing/model-ensemble tuning has diminishing returns; prioritize train-derived validation or a qualitatively different online adaptation idea.

description: Run 036 n=5 order-dependent smoothing model mixture

asi.hypothesis: Using half-count smoothing for lower-order models and alpha 0.3 only for order-4 contexts may preserve n=4 calibration while keeping sharper n=5 signal.
asi.source: runs 018-022 n=4 half-count optimum, runs 031-033 n=5 global alpha bracket, runs 034-035 failed adaptive/ensemble smoothing, lectures 5/13/15 on mismatch, repeated histories, and robust mixtures
asi.learned: Order-dependent smoothing scored 2.9755466270, worse than global alpha 0.3. The n=5 improvement is not isolated to order-4 smoothing; sharpening lower/intermediate orders or maintaining a consistent alpha across the recursive mixture appears important.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened by about 0.000609 bits/symbol versus current best global alpha 0.3.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid further smoothing tweaks. Prefer current n=5 alpha 0.3 model, then explore qualitatively different online adaptation/recency or build train-derived validation.

description: Run 037 recent-window online mixture crash due dataclass import issue

asi.hypothesis: A small ramped recent-window online model mixed into the current best n=5 alpha 0.3 predictor may capture local realization-specific structure.
asi.source: run 036 next hint to explore recency, runs 006/017 online adaptation evidence, ideas backlog recency-window adaptation, lectures 5/13/15
asi.learned: The predictor did not import because a local @dataclass(slots=True) in a module loaded via importlib.util.module_from_spec triggered a Python dataclasses sys.modules lookup error before evaluation. The modeling idea was not tested.
asi.rollback_reason: Crash before benchmark; no valid bits_per_symbol or evaluated tokens.
asi.next_action_hint: Retry the same recent-window hypothesis without dataclasses, e.g. use a simple slotted/manual count class or plain lists/dicts.

description: Run 038 n=5 alpha 0.3 with ramped recent-window mixture

asi.hypothesis: A small ramped recent-window online model mixed into the current best n=5 alpha 0.3 predictor may capture local realization-specific structure without dominating the train+online model.
asi.source: run 037 crash retry without dataclasses; run 036 next hint to explore recency; runs 006/017 online adaptation evidence; autoresearch ideas backlog; lectures 5/13/15 on mismatch, recent repeated history, and universal mixtures
asi.learned: The recent-window mixture improved to 2.9740425033, about 0.000895 bits/symbol better than the prior best. A small separate online-only window captures useful local structure that cumulative train+online counts miss, unlike global online overweighting.
asi.next_focus: Bracket the recency mechanism coarsely: try smaller recent weight/window or a weight 0.03/0.08, but avoid excessive public-practice tuning; validate the recency gain soon.

asi.hypothesis: If the Run 038 recent component was underweighted, raising the ramped max recent weight from 0.05 to 0.08 should improve; otherwise it will overfit short-window noise.
asi.source: run 038 recent-window improvement; runs 033-036 smoothing saturation; run 017 global online overweighting failure; lectures 5/13/15 on mismatch, recent repeated history, and universal mixtures
